title: gleditsia horrida subsp. caspica
intro: Gleditsia horrida subsp. caspica, also known as the Caspian gleditsia, is a plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae, native to the Caspian region of Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues. The herbal preparations of Gleditsia horrida subsp. caspica include infusions, decoctions, and powders, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and other medicinal products.
